ours.title: Investigations
ours.label: Warner Bros. Records
ours.albumDNA: Brad Mehldau's Investigations, released on Warner Bros. Records in 1999, is a post-bop recording. The program includes "The Very Thought of You", "Sehnsucht", and "Young at Heart". Established Mehldau as the most important jazz pianist of his generation. His classical influences brought new depth to the piano trio.

ours.albumDNA: No Parking Tow Away Zone (2014) captures Kenny Garrett exploring jazz fusion territory. The date includes "Old Folks" and "Wanderlust". A jazz fusion entry from 2014 by Kenny Garrett, documenting the breadth of the contemporary period.

ours.title: Tokyo 1981
ours.label: Elemental Music
ours.albumDNA: Released on Elemental Music in 2018, Tokyo 1981 is a compelling hard bop statement by Woody Shaw. Highlights include 'Rosewood' and ''Round Midnight'.

ours.title: Amaryllis / Belladonna
ours.label: Nonesuch
ours.albumDNA: Guitarist Mary Halvorson's Nonesuch debut is a ravishing double album pairing her octet with string arrangements by Robert Moody. Her signature bent notes and fractured melodies are set against lush orchestral textures, creating a sound world both disorienting and deeply beautiful. Marked Halvorson's arrival as a major composer working at the intersection of jazz, rock, and contemporary classical music. The album earned a Pulitzer Prize finalist nomination and widespread critical acclaim.

ours.title: The Empress of the Blues
ours.label: Various
ours.albumDNA: The Empress of the Blues stands among Bessie Smith's most celebrated works, capturing the essential spirit of Early Jazz. The Empress of the Blues remains a landmark of Early Jazz, cementing Bessie Smith's reputation as one of the defining voices of the era and earning consistent praise from critics worldwide.
